Stripe and Advent Reportedly Offer $53 Billion to Acquire PayPal According to Reuters, Stripe and Advent International have offered to acquire PayPal for $60.50 per share, valuing the payments giant at more than $53 billion. The offer is backed by around $50 billion in committed financing and represents a roughly 28% premium to PayPal's latest closing price. Sources said the parties are aiming to reach an agreement by the end of July, though there is no certainty a deal will be completed. Stripe has increasingly focused on stablecoin infrastructure and crypto payments, while PayPal launched PYUSD in 2023 and has become one of the largest regulated stablecoin issuers.
US freezes $131M in Iran-linked crypto as Middle East tensions rise
queue.summary: “US Treasury is committed to disrupting and degrading Iran’s illicit financial activities, including its abuse of digital assets,” US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday.

queue.title: Coinbase Executive Says 95%-100% of Company Code Is Now AI-Assisted, Up From 40% in February Rob Witoff, Head of Platform at Coinbase, stated in Cointelegraph interview that 95% to 100% of Coinbase's code is currently written by or with the assistance of AI and large language models (LLMs). This figure represents a significant increase from the 40% estimated in February. Witoff noted that effectively 100% of employees use AI daily, with most engineers running 5 to 10 AI agents simultaneously; collectively, these agents are performing the coding work equivalent to about 1,200 employees. Coinbase projects that by 2030, AI agents could be performing the work equivalent to 100,000 employees.
Stripe, Advent offer $53B to acquire PayPal: Report
queue.summary: Stripe and Advent reportedly offered to buy PayPal for $53 billion, at a 28% premium compared to the company’s Tuesday closing price.

queue.title: Japanese Financial Giant Downgrades Circle to Underperform, Cuts Price Target From $85 to $50 Japanese investment bank Mizuho downgraded Circle from Neutral to Underperform and cut its price target from $85 to $50. The bank said OpenUSD’s model of distributing most reserve income to issuers and distribution partners could force Circle to share more revenue with partners, putting pressure on its margins. As a result, Mizuho raised its forecast for Circle’s 2027 distribution and transaction expense ratio from 64% to 73%, and lowered its adjusted EBITDA forecast from $1.09 billion to $699 million.
JUST IN: Stripe and Advent offer to buy PayPal $PYPL for $53 billion, Reuters reports.

Pumpfun Completes First Team and Investor Token Unlock Worth $86.49 Million PUMP According to @EmberCN, Pumpfun's one-year lock-up for team and investor tokens expired today, marking the start of a three-year vesting period. Earlier today, Pumpfun completed its first team and investor token unlock, transferring and distributing 57.279 billion PUMP tokens, worth approximately $86.49 million, to 121 wallets.
